input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
Your `.testsettings` file is a legacy format that's **not supported** by modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate it to a `.runsettings` file. Here's what your settings map to:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property (30000) | `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| `AssemblyResolution` / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` (usually unnecessary now) |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` | `<DataCollector>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Deployment is automatic in SDK-style projects; disable if not needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the legacy CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Include>
                <ModulePath>.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Include>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*moq\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file that configures:
1. Test deployment/assembly resolution
2. Test timeout (30000ms)
3. Code coverage data collector

They need to migrate this to a `.runsettings` file. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and not supported by modern MSTest. Let me invoke the MSTest migration skill for the best guidance, and also create the `.runsettings` file.

Let me invoke the migrate-mstest-v1v2-to-v3 skill since this is about `.testsettings` migration which is part of the v1/v2 to v3 migration path.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Deployment is automatic in SDK-style projects; disable if not needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the legacy CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Include>
                <ModulePath>.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Include>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*moq\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire up the `.runsettings` in your project file so it's used automatically:

Here's a summary of the migration:

**What changed:**

1. **`local.runsettings`** — new file replacing `local.testsettings` with equivalent settings:
   - **Timeout**: `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` (per-method) + `<TestSessionTimeout>` (session-level)
   - **Deployment**: Disabled — SDK-style projects handle assembly resolution automatically
   - **Code Coverage**: Updated `DataCollector` URI to `2.0` format

2. **`TestProject.csproj`** — added `<RunSettingsFilePath>` so tests pick up the settings automatically

**Next steps:**
- You can safely **delete `local.testsettings`** — it's no longer read by MSTest v3
- Run with: `dotnet test` (or `dotnet test -s local.runsettings` to be explicit)
- For code coverage collection, also pass `--collect "Code Coverage"` on the CLI
